meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: HARD MANDATE (NO EXCEPTIONS): For EVERY bug/regression, you MUST (1) replicate with HIGH CONFIDENCE via a failing test, (2) validate that replication FAILS via make target, and ONLY THEN (3) apply a fix. No exceptions.
ANTI-HALLUCINATION CONSTRAINT: Do NOT invent root causes or describe nonexistent logic. If you have not read the relevant code paths, add an investigation task.
TOOLING REQUIREMENT: All verification MUST use the make-based workflow. Cross-platform checks MUST pass (make all). macOS-only integration tests via project.mk targets.
RULE OF TWO (COMMIT GATE): No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent strict reviews confirm correctness.
INDEFINITE CYCLE: The task list must never become empty. Always expand scope towards True Perfection.

REARCHITECTURE IN PROGRESS: The monolith pr_split_script.js (6225 lines) is being split into 14 chunks loaded in sequence. Old implementation archived in scratch/archive/pr-split-v1/. Reference the archive when extracting chunks — do not hallucinate behavior.

END-TO-END VALIDATION MANDATORY: Mock MCP tests are necessary but INSUFFICIENT. Real Claude integration test (make integration-test-prsplit) MUST pass. The implementation must work when users run `osm pr-split -claude-command=ollama -claude-arg=launch ...`.

osm:pty DISTINCTION: The JavaScript module 'osm:pty' (registered in internal/builtin/register.go, defined in internal/builtin/pty/module.go) is UNUSED dead code — safe to remove. The underlying Go package internal/builtin/pty/ is CRITICAL — used by claudemux for PTY spawning. DO NOT delete the Go package.

CHUNK EXTRACTION ORDER: Chunks MUST be extracted sequentially (01→02→...→13) because later chunks depend on symbols from earlier chunks. Each chunk test must load all prerequisite chunks via loadChunkEngine(t, overrides, "00_core", "01_analysis", ...). NEVER test a chunk in isolation from its dependencies.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Execute tasks strictly in sequence. Update blueprint.json incrementally after each task.
mandatoryDirectives.sourceOfTruth: Archive reference: scratch/archive/pr-split-v1/ (the original monolith, 6225 lines). Live chunks: internal/command/pr_split_00_core.js through pr_split_13_tui.js. Go bridge: internal/command/pr_split.go. Tests: internal/command/pr_split_*_test.go. Chunk test helper: loadChunkEngine() in pr_split_00_core_test.go. Monolith test helpers: loadPrSplitEngine()/loadPrSplitEngineWithEval() in pr_split_test.go. TUI: internal/termmux/ui/autosplit.go (AutoSplitModel). Design doc: docs/architecture-pr-split-chunks.md.
mandatoryDirectives.fullEndToEndIntegrationTesting: The -integration flag, -claude-command, -claude-arg flags registered in internal/command/main_test.go TestMain. project.mk targets: integration-test-prsplit, integration-test-prsplit-mcp, integration-test-termmux. New test functions in internal/command/ matching TestIntegration_* are automatically picked up by make integration-test-prsplit-mcp.
mandatoryDirectives.chunkArchitecture: 14 JS chunk files loaded in order by pr_split.go (loadChunkedScript). Each chunk is an IIFE that attaches exports to globalThis.prSplit (initialized by chunk 00). Chunks may reference symbols from earlier chunks via globalThis.prSplit. Go-injected globals (autoSplitTUI, tuiMux, splitView, planEditorFactory, prSplitConfig) are available to all chunks. Toggle via PR_SPLIT_CHUNKED=1 env var (c.useChunkedScript field in pr_split.go).

statusSection.currentState: Planning phase complete. Chunk 00 extracted and tested (~400 lines, 15+ tests via loadChunkEngine). Chunks 01-13 are stubs (~8 lines each, IIFE wrapper + TODO). Monolith functional as default (PR_SPLIT_CHUNKED=1 to use chunks). Integration tests baseline verified (make all PASSES, make integration-test-prsplit-mcp PASSES). Git clean on main.
statusSection.goalState: pr_split_script.js monolith replaced by 14 independently-testable chunk files. Each chunk is under 500 lines. Go code provides reusable modules, JS provides app-specific logic. End-to-end validation passes with REAL Claude. Terminal output is clean (no mangling). Process exits cleanly on q/Ctrl+C. make all green cross-platform. Every line has a reason to exist.

Blueprint rewritten with per-chunk function extraction details, dependency chains, behavioral acceptance criteria, and expansion scope.

sequentialTasks.description: Chunk 00 fully populated from archive lines 1-292: module imports (osm:bt, osm:exec, osm:text/template, osm:sharedStateSymbols, osm:os, osm:lipgloss), style helpers, constants, runtime config, gitExec, shellQuote, gitAddChangedFiles, dirname, fileExtension, sanitizeBranchName, padIndex, discoverVerifyCommand, scopedVerifyCommand, isCancelled/isPaused/isForceCancelled. Test file: pr_split_00_core_test.go with loadChunkEngine() helper.

sequentialTasks.task: R04.1: Integration test baseline verification
sequentialTasks.description: Run make all and make integration-test-prsplit-mcp to verify the baseline before proceeding with chunk extraction. Confirms: monolith loads, test infrastructure works, mock MCP pipeline completes.

sequentialTasks.task: R05: Extract pr_split_01_analysis.js — analyzeDiff, analyzeDiffStats
sequentialTasks.description: From archive (scratch/archive/pr-split-v1/pr_split_script.js lines 293-445), extract into internal/command/pr_split_01_analysis.js: (1) analyzeDiff(config) — calls gitExec('diff', '--stat', ...) and gitExec('diff', '--name-status', ...) to produce {files: [{name, status, additions, deletions}], stats: {filesChanged, insertions, deletions}, raw: string}. (2) analyzeDiffStats(config) — lightweight stats-only variant. Both reference prSplit._gitExec from chunk 00. IIFE wrapper: (function(prSplit) { ... })(globalThis.prSplit). Attach as prSplit.analyzeDiff and prSplit.analyzeDiffStats. Create test file: internal/command/pr_split_01_analysis_test.go using loadChunkEngine(t, overrides, "00_core", "01_analysis"). Tests MUST use a real git repo (t.TempDir() + git init + commits) not mocked gitExec, to verify the diff parsing handles edge cases (binary files, renames, deletions).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: pr_split_01_analysis.js exports analyzeDiff and analyzeDiffStats on globalThis.prSplit. Tests with real git repos prove: (1) multi-file diff parsed correctly (count, names, status letters). (2) Binary files included in stats. (3) Renames detected (R status). (4) Error returned when base branch doesn't exist. (5) Empty diff returns zero counts. Tests pass via loadChunkEngine loading chunks 00+01. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R06: Extract pr_split_02_grouping.js — grouping strategies + selection
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 446-873, extract into internal/command/pr_split_02_grouping.js: (1) groupByDirectory(files, config) — groups by top-level directory. (2) groupByExtension(files, config) — groups by file extension. (3) groupByPattern(files, config) — groups by regex patterns from config.patterns. (4) groupByChunks(files, config) — splits into N roughly-equal chunks. (5) parseGoImports(content) — extracts import paths from Go source. (6) detectGoModulePath(config) — reads go.mod for module path. (7) groupByDependency(files, config) — Go-specific grouping by shared imports. (8) applyStrategy(strategy, files, config) — dispatches to correct groupBy* function. (9) selectStrategy(files, config) — auto-detects best strategy (Go projects → dependency, many dirs → directory, else → extension). All attach to globalThis.prSplit. Dependencies: prSplit._dirname, prSplit._fileExtension from chunk 00. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_02_grouping_test.go, load chunks 00+01+02.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 9 functions exported on globalThis.prSplit. Tests prove: (1) groupByDirectory produces correct groups for known file lists. (2) groupByExtension groups .go/.js/.md files correctly. (3) groupByPattern matches configured regex. (4) groupByChunks produces N groups. (5) parseGoImports extracts standard+third-party imports. (6) selectStrategy returns 'dependency' for Go repos, 'directory' for 5+ top-level dirs. (7) applyStrategy dispatches correctly. (8) Empty file lists return empty groups, not errors. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R07: Extract pr_split_03_planning.js — createSplitPlan, savePlan, loadPlan
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 874-924 and 1156-1273, extract into internal/command/pr_split_03_planning.js: (1) createSplitPlan(groups, config) — creates {version: 2, splits: [{name, description, files: []}], baseBranch, branchPrefix} using sanitizeBranchName and padIndex from chunk 00. (2) savePlan(plan, config) — writes JSON to .pr-split-plan.json via osmod.writeFile (prSplit._modules.os). (3) loadPlan(config) — reads plan file, handles V1→V2 migration (old format had flat file lists, new has split objects), handles missing/corrupt files gracefully. Dependencies: prSplit._sanitizeBranchName, prSplit._padIndex, prSplit._modules.os from chunk 00; prSplit.selectStrategy from chunk 02 (used as fallback). Create test: internal/command/pr_split_03_planning_test.go, load chunks 00-03.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exports createSplitPlan, savePlan, loadPlan. Tests prove: (1) createSplitPlan generates valid branch names from group names. (2) savePlan + loadPlan round-trips correctly. (3) V1 plan file (old format) migrates to V2 on load. (4) Corrupt JSON returns error, not panic. (5) Missing file returns null/empty, not error. (6) Branch names sanitized (no spaces, slashes escaped). make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R08: Extract pr_split_04_validation.js — all validate* functions
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 925-1155, extract into internal/command/pr_split_04_validation.js: (1) validateClassification(classification) → {valid: bool, errors: string[]} — checks classification has groups array, each group has name+files. (2) validatePlan(plan) → {valid, errors} — checks plan structure, non-empty splits, unique branch names. (3) validateSplitPlan(plan, diff) → {valid, errors} — cross-validates plan against actual diff (all changed files accounted for, no file in multiple splits). (4) validateResolution(resolution) → {valid, errors} — validates conflict resolution has strategy+changes. All pure functions with no external dependencies beyond JS builtins. Attach to globalThis.prSplit. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_04_validation_test.go, load chunks 00-04.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 4 validators exported with consistent {valid, errors} interface. Tests cover: (1) validateClassification rejects missing groups, empty files. (2) validatePlan rejects duplicate branch names, empty splits. (3) validateSplitPlan detects unaccounted files and overlapping files. (4) validateResolution rejects missing strategy. (5) All return valid=true for correct inputs. (6) Error messages are descriptive (contain field names). make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R09: Extract pr_split_05_execution.js — executeSplit
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 1274-1444, extract into internal/command/pr_split_05_execution.js: executeSplit(plan, config) — iterates plan.splits, for each: (1) Gets base SHA via gitExec('rev-parse', baseBranch). (2) Creates branch from SHA (not branch name) via gitExec('checkout', '-b', branchName, baseSHA). (3) For each file in split: if status='D' → gitExec('rm', file), else → gitExec('checkout', sourceBranch, '--', file) + gitExec('add', file). (4) Commits with descriptive message. (5) Returns to original branch. (6) Reports per-split success/failure. Dependencies: prSplit._gitExec, prSplit.validatePlan from chunks 00, 04. Uses prSplit._isCancelled for cancellation. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_05_execution_test.go with REAL git repos (t.TempDir, git init, create files, commit, create diff). Load chunks 00-05.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: executeSplit exported. Tests with real git prove: (1) Creates correct number of branches from base SHA. (2) Each branch contains only its assigned files. (3) Deleted files handled via git rm (D status). (4) Returns to original branch after execution. (5) Per-split error reporting works (bad file → error for that split, others continue). (6) Cancellation via isCancelled stops processing. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R10: Extract pr_split_06_verification.js — verify, equivalence, cleanup
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 1445-1736, extract into internal/command/pr_split_06_verification.js: (1) verifySplit(split, config) — runs verify command (e.g. make test) on a single split branch, returns {passed, output, error}. Uses prSplit._scopedVerifyCommand from chunk 00 and prSplit._modules.exec. (2) verifySplits(plan, config) — runs verifySplit for all splits, respects isCancelled. (3) verifyEquivalence(plan, config) — verifies combined diff of all splits equals original diff. (4) verifyEquivalenceDetailed(plan, config) — file-level equivalence with per-file results. (5) cleanupBranches(plan, config) — deletes split branches, idempotent. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_06_verification_test.go, load chunks 00-06.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 5 functions exported. Tests prove: (1) verifySplit returns passed=true when verify command exits 0, passed=false with output on failure. (2) verifySplits cancellation stops remaining checks. (3) verifyEquivalence detects mismatch when a file is missing from splits. (4) cleanupBranches deletes branches, second call is no-op. (5) Missing verify command produces clear error. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R11: Extract pr_split_07_prcreation.js — createPRs
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 1737-1852, extract into internal/command/pr_split_07_prcreation.js: createPRs(plan, config) — for each split: (1) Pushes branch via gitExec('push', 'origin', branchName). (2) Creates PR via exec('gh', 'pr', 'create', ...) with --title, --body, --base (stacking: first PR bases on config.baseBranch, subsequent base on prior split branch). (3) Supports --draft flag. (4) Returns per-PR results. Dependencies: prSplit._gitExec, prSplit._modules.exec, prSplit._padIndex from chunk 00. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_07_prcreation_test.go with mock exec, load chunks 00-07.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: createPRs exported. Tests prove: (1) Correct stacking order (PR 2 bases on branch 1). (2) --draft flag when config.draft=true. (3) Per-branch error reporting (push failure → error for that PR, others proceed). (4) Missing gh CLI produces clear error. (5) PR body includes split description. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R12: Extract pr_split_08_conflict.js — AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES, resolveConflicts
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 1853-2331, extract into internal/command/pr_split_08_conflict.js: (1) AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES — object with 5 named strategies (e.g. ours, theirs, union, go-imports, package-json), each with {detect: fn, fix: fn, description: string}. (2) resolveConflicts(plan, config) — iterates splits, detects conflicts via git merge --no-commit, attempts local auto-fix strategies, tracks results. Note: resolveConflictsWithClaude is in chunk 09, referenced via late-binding (prSplit.resolveConflictsWithClaude) when local strategies fail. Dependencies: prSplit._gitExec, prSplit._modules.exec, prSplit._isCancelled from chunk 00. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_08_conflict_test.go, load chunks 00-08.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: AUTO_FIX_STRATEGIES and resolveConflicts exported. Tests prove: (1) Each strategy's detect function correctly identifies its conflict type. (2) Each strategy's fix function produces valid resolution. (3) resolveConflicts tries strategies in order, stops on first success. (4) Unresolvable conflicts escalate (result.escalated=true). (5) Late-binding to chunk 09 resolveConflictsWithClaude: test verifies it's called when set, gracefully skipped when not. make all passes.

(5) resolveConflictsWithClaude with mock executor parses a hardcoded Claude response. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R14: Extract pr_split_10_pipeline.js — automatedSplit orchestration
sequentialTasks.description: From archive lines 2728-4049, extract into internal/command/pr_split_10_pipeline.js. This is the largest chunk (~1300 lines). Functions: (1) AUTOMATED_DEFAULTS — default config values for automated mode. (2) sendToHandle(handle, text) — async, writes text then newline as SEPARATE writes (the T04a fix — critical for PTY). (3) waitForLogged(indicator, timeout) — polls log.searchLogs for expected output. (4) automatedSplit(config) — the main async pipeline: analyze → classify (via Claude) → plan → execute → verify → equivalence → create PRs. Orchestrates chunks 01-09 functions via globalThis.prSplit late-binding. (5) heuristicFallback(diff, config) — non-AI grouping+planning when Claude unavailable. (6) classificationToGroups(classification) — converts Claude classification response to group objects. (7) cleanupExecutor(executor) — teardown. Dependencies: ALL prior chunks via late-binding through globalThis.prSplit. Create test: internal/command/pr_split_10_pipeline_test.go, load chunks 00-10.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All pipeline functions exported. Tests prove: (1) sendToHandle writes text and newline separately (mock handle records two write calls). (2) heuristicFallback produces a valid plan from raw diff stats. (3) classificationToGroups converts classification JSON to group array. (4) automatedSplit with mock executor/mock Claude completes all pipeline stages (analyze→classify→plan→execute→verify→equivalence). (5) Pipeline cancellation via isCancelled stops cleanly. make all passes.

sequentialTasks.task: R20: Post-monolith integration gate — make integration-test-prsplit-mcp
sequentialTasks.description: Run make integration-test-prsplit-mcp after monolith removal (R19). This runs TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP which exercises the full automated pipeline with a mock MCP Claude. If it fails: (1) Identify which pipeline stage fails. (2) Trace to the specific chunk function. (3) Fix in the chunk. (4) Re-run until green.

sequentialTasks.task: R27: Real Claude integration test — make integration-test-prsplit
sequentialTasks.description: Run make integration-test-prsplit with configured AI provider. This exercises REAL PTY spawn, REAL prompt submission via sendToHandle (text + newline as separate writes), REAL response parsing, REAL pipeline completion. If TestIntegration_AutoSplit_RealE2E doesn't exist, create it in internal/command/pr_split_integration_test.go with: (1) skipIfNoClaude(t) guard. (2) Real git repo setup with a non-trivial diff (5+ files, 2+ languages). (3) Full automatedSplit pipeline execution. (4) Verify: Claude response parsed, plan created, splits executed, terminal output clean (no ANSI mangling), process exits cleanly. This is THE test Hana demanded. To be clear, prior to splitting up the implementation, the implementation was completely and utterly broken, per `scratch/pr-split-auto-split-completely-broken.md`. It is HIGHLY UNLIKELY to be any better by this point - if anything, you've likely broken it MORE. There were clearly some things that went EXTREMELY fucky when the behavior of the script was migrated to use async/await. It is mandatory to use async/awake, but no doubt you fucked it up. There are other-many-unrelated bugs. You just haven't identified them yet. Plus, probably, all the issues in scratch/pr-split-auto-split-completely-broken.md . You will need to spec out a HOLISTIC approach to COMPLETELY REWORK the behavior, and make it feasible to _reliably prove it behaves correctly. The most significant problem, for you, is that you are a lazy, incompetent husband. Gambate ne, you piece of shit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Task expanded through REPEATED subagent-driven investigations to come up with a multi-pronged maximum-reliability plan to avoid a disaster as described by `scratch/pr-split-auto-split-completely-broken.md` - a direct result of your past and ongoing incompetence. You need to do better than all existing automated testing attempts, you need to be _far_ more reliable at actually identifying flaws in the end to end behavior. It is not sufficient to unit test or integration test components without a real claude - not even close. The PTY output MUST be verified. You MUST ensure that there is a 'screenshot' mechanism to copy the visibile content as text. You MUST ensure that you can reliably observe ALL RELEVANT OUTPUT to ensure that the output is ACTUALLY SANE. This functionality needs to be carefully architected into the termmux package, and exposed by way of coherent, performant, well-designed MODULAR and sensible API surface. Use subagents to perform extensive solution-analysis-master style planning and Rule of Two review and refinement, prior to making any changes. Utilize this functionality in integration tests - existing as appropriate, or new. Strengthen ALL validation of input and output over TTY, both the runtime behavior AND the test behavior. Ensure that you ACTUALLY TEST all functionality, both in a manner that will be caught by CI (not depending on a real claude) and locally (depending on a real claude, per project.mk). You MUST keep running the real-claude tests for the duration of the session, as part of your normal validation process. All subagent concerns enumerated AND actioned, methodically. All subagent ideation on BETTER WAYS OF WORKING better ways to PREVENT REGRESSION and _reliably_ resolve all issues, are prioritised and actioned. Real Claude integration test passes end-to-end. DEEP validation of state - PROVE it actually works properly, absolute ban on SHALLOW assertions. ALL potential flaws investigated addressed. Pipeline completes: analyze → classify → plan → execute. Process exits cleanly (no hung goroutines). The original bugs in scratch/pr-split-auto-split-completely-broken.md are verified fixed WITH THE HIGHEST DEGREE OF CONFIDENCE - public humiliation is in the cards if you fail to achieve this, anata. make integration-test-prsplit exits 0 - that is the most basic of requirements, and INSUFFICIENT by itself.
sequentialTasks.additionalDescription: HANA DIRECTIVE (config.mk intervention): The unit-test-level screenshot/observation APIs (Steps(), OutputLines(), getView) are FINE. But the ACTUAL requirement is integration tests that run REAL `osm pr-split` as a subprocess via termmux-controlled PTY, with REAL Claude, and use VTerm.String() to capture/assert on the ACTUAL multiplexed terminal UI state. This is NOT about JS engine mocks — it's about running the actual CLI binary, spawning it inside termmux, observing the full muxed output (BubbleTea TUI + Claude passthrough), and proving it's sane. Implementation plan: (1) Build `osm` binary for tests. (2) Spawn it inside a termmux.Mux-controlled PTY with real Claude config. (3) After pipeline completes (or times out), capture VTerm screenshot via ChildExitOutput() or VTerm.String(). (4) Assert: step progression visible, no ANSI mangling, Claude output clean, process exits cleanly. (5) Also strengthen existing real-Claude tests (TestIntegration_AutoSplitWithClaude_Pipeline) with VTerm output capture. Keep the unit-level observation APIs too — they serve a complementary purpose.
sequentialTasks.additionalAcceptance: Integration test exists that runs REAL `osm pr-split` as a subprocess inside a termmux-controlled PTY with real Claude. VTerm.String() used to capture terminal state at key points (post-analysis, post-Claude-response, post-split, post-completion). Assertions verify: (a) Steps appear in correct order in terminal output. (b) No ANSI escape sequence garbage. (c) Claude was spawned (toggle prompt visible or classification received). (d) Process exits cleanly within timeout. (e) All broken.md issues verified fixed with HIGH CONFIDENCE via VTerm observation. make integration-test-prsplit exits 0. Unit-level observation APIs (getView/getSteps/getOutputLines) also wired with complementary tests.

sequentialTasks.task: R41 — Chunk architecture ADR
sequentialTasks.description: Write docs/adr/001-pr-split-chunked-architecture.md: Context (why monolith was untenable), Decision (14 IIFE chunks with sequential loading), Consequences (independent testability, loading order overhead), Alternatives (CJS modules — annoying to wire up, single-file sections — status quo). The chunking strategy is basically just a compromise to have the implementation be something 'able to be implemented from scratch without it being baked in' i.e. be indicative of what a user could have implemented as a consumer of the compiled osm binary as a custom variant, which, typically, would be easiest to distribute as a single script. Chunked script being the closes to those semantics, basically. Do note that this might be walked back in favor of a better-conceived CJS-native approach.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: ADR exists with Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives. Referenced from docs/architecture.md. Provides rationale for future maintainers.

continuousVerification.task: After EVERY significant change, run make pipeline and integration tests
continuousVerification.description: Run `make all` after every code change. Run `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` after pr-split changes. Run `make integration-test-prsplit` periodically for real Claude validation. Verify chunk file sizes stay ≤ 500 lines.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY check fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix. Zero tolerance for flaky tests.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: Do not claim a regression is fixed without test evidence.
No commits until two contiguous, issue-free subagent reviews confirm correctness.
The old monolith is in scratch/archive/pr-split-v1/. Reference it when extracting chunks. Do NOT hallucinate function signatures or behavior — READ the archive.

Each chunk extraction (R05-R17) must: (1) Read the archive source lines. (2) Write the IIFE-wrapped chunk. (3) Attach exports to globalThis.prSplit. (4) Create test file using loadChunkEngine with all prerequisite chunks. (5) Run make all before proceeding to next chunk.
